title: ETH - Insufficient funds | ERC20 tokens, USDT

actions.value:
In order to send an Ethereum token (ERC-20), for example USDT, USDC, DAI and other ERC-20 tokens, you first need some ETH to pay the gas (or mining) fees. This is an Ethereum policy, not an Edge one. We receive no profit from these gas fees. They are paid solely to Ethereum network validators to process your transaction on-chain.

ERC-20 tokens *typically* can NOT pay their own fees.

 

In the v4.10 update of Edge that was released on August 8th 2024, you can now swap between tokens on the Ethereum without initially requiring some ETH. If you need to send or do a cross-chain swap, you will still require some ETH to cover gas fees for these kinds of transactions.

 

For example, if you have USDC that was stuck in a 0 ETH wallet, you can now swap into another Ethereum-based token using "gasless swaps" powered by 0x - or swap for some ETH so you may send or trade your remaining tokens to meet your goals. Note: Not all tokens are supported. For example, USDT is not compatible with gasless swaps, so you may still need to buy or transfer ETH into this wallet to be able to move or use the USDT.
 

Please be aware that gas prices can sharply rise when Ethereum becomes congested. You can check the current 'Estimated Cost of Transaction Actions' on https://etherscan.io/gastracker
 

Please let us know if you have any more questions.
